title: Capability security — audit logging and violation reporting
purpose: Capability violations should be observable — operators need to know when a process attempts an operation it's not allowed to perform. This brief adds audit logging for capability checks and a violation callback.
task: Add audit logging for capability enforcement.

requirements.title: Capability audit log
requirements.spec: Log all capability checks (granted and denied) to an observable channel.
acceptance: CapabilityAuditEvent: { pid, capability, operation, granted: bool }
Audit events emitted for every NIF capability check
Audit log is optional (behind a feature flag or runtime toggle)
Test: enable audit logging, call NIF, verify audit event emitted

requirements.title: Violation callback
requirements.spec: A configurable callback invoked on capability violations.
acceptance: Default: log to stderr. Configurable: custom handler
Handler receives full context: pid, operation, required capability, process capabilities
Test: custom violation handler collects events, verify on denied NIF call

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement audit log persistence — in-memory or callback only
SHALL NOT implement real-time alerting — that's operator tooling built on the callback
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
